zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Somery於1290年出生在英國武斯特斯爾伍斯特時，她的父親Roger de Somery III35歲，母親Lady Agnes Lucia de Pagnel35歲。她於1302年與John Sutton I在英國英格蘭諾廷加姆爾華索普結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1384年在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾杜德利享年94歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭韋斯特 米德藍茲杜德利杜德利 卡素。

nameMeanings.name: Somery
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Abelino, Adela, Alberto, Alfredo, Ana, Angel, Asuncion, Domingo, Elena, Emiliano, Emilio, Eusebio.
Spanish: possibly a habitational name from La Somera in Cuenca province, named from somero ‘shallow’. This surname is most common in the Philippines.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Somery was born in 1290, in Worcester, Worcestershire, England, her father, Roger de Somery III, was 35 and her mother, Lady Agnes Lucia de Pagnel, was 35. She married John Sutton I in 1302, in Warsop, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 4 daughters. She died in 1384, in Dudley, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 94, and was buried in Dudley Castle, Dudley, West Midlands, England, United Kingdom.

Elle a épousé John Sutton I en 1302, à Warsop, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée en 1384, à Dudley, Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 94 ans, et a été enterrée à Dudley Castle, Dudley, West Midlands, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ko.Dudley Castle Crypt, Dudley, Metropolita: 영국 잉글랜드 웨스트 미들랜드즈 드들리 드들리 카스틀
ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Somery(이)가 1290년 영국 워스스터실르 워스스터에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Roger de Somery III(은)는 35세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Agnes Lucia de Pagnel(은)는 35세였다. 그녀는 1302년 영국 잉글랜드 노틴그함실르 워소프에서 John Sutton I(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1384년 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르 드들리에서 94세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 웨스트 미들랜드즈 드들리 드들리 카스틀에 묻혔다.

Ela casou-se com John Sutton I em 1302, em Warsop, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 4 filhas. ru.Dudley Castle Crypt, Dudley, Metropolita: дудли кастл, дудли, вест мидландс, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Somery родилась в 1290 г. в местности ворсестир, ворсестиршир, Англия, её отцу по имени Roger de Somery III было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Agnes Lucia de Pagnel, 35. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Sutton I в 1302 г. в местности варсоп, ноттингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 4 дочери. Она умерла в 1384 г. в местности дудли, стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 94 лет и была похоронена в местности дудли кастл, дудли, вест мидландс, Англия, Великобритания.
